title: Watering the garden with the overflow
subtitle: A short visualisation for building the felt sense of directing abundance generously and without depletion.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation from Day 42 of the MONEY program. The scene: a garden, a full water butt, and the calm of having more than enough to share without running dry.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

practice.whenToUse: Use to build the felt sense of directing abundance generously, without fear that giving depletes the source.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from the Day 42 theme. The image is a garden: full, growing, and needing exactly the overflow you have.
Sit or lie down. Close your eyes. One slow breath.
Picture a garden. It is yours. There are things growing in it that need water. To your right there is a large water butt — full to the brim after rain. You have more water than you need.

You pick up the watering can and fill it. You water one bed, then another. The water falls steadily. You go back to the butt and fill the can again. The butt is still nearly full. The giving does not drain it.
Notice the ease of this. There is no calculation of how much is left. There is no rationing. The overflow goes where it is needed and the source stays full.
This is the feel of financial overflow: enough to direct, enough to share, enough that the directing does not deplete you. Let that feeling settle in the body.
Hold that image for two minutes. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.
